title.zh: 卷八十五 谷永杜鄴傳
title.en: Volume 85: Gu Yong and Du Ye
title.raw: 卷八十五 谷永杜鄴傳 Volume 85: Gu Yong and Du Ye

sentences.zh: 卷八十五 谷永杜鄴傳第五十五

translations.literal: Volume 85, Biography the Fifty-Fifth: Gu Yong and Du Ye.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 85, the fifty-fifth biography: Gu Yong and Du Ye.

sentences.zh: 谷永字子雲，長安人也。

translations.literal: Gu Yong, courtesy name Ziyun, was a man of Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong, styled Ziyun, was a native of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 父吉，為衛司馬，使送郅支單于侍子，為郅支所殺，語在《陳湯傳》。

translations.literal: His father Ji was guard marshal, sent to escort the heir of the Chanyu Zhizhi, and was killed by Zhizhi—the account is in the "Biography of Chen Tang".
translations.idiomatic: His father Ji served as guard marshal on the mission to escort Zhizhi's heir and died at Zhizhi's hands, as told in the biography of Chen Tang.

sentences.zh: 永少為長安小史，後博學經書。

translations.literal: Yong in youth was a petty clerk of Chang'an; afterward he broadly studied the classics and documents.
translations.idiomatic: He began as a petty clerk in Chang'an, then immersed himself in the canon.

sentences.zh: 建昭中，御史大夫繁延壽聞其有茂材，除補屬，舉為太常丞，數上疏言得失。

translations.literal: In the Jianzhao period, Imperial Counselor Fan Yanshou heard he had outstanding talent, appointed him as subordinate, recommended him as aide to the chamberlain of ceremonials, and he several times submitted memorials on gain and loss.
translations.idiomatic: Under Jianzhao, Imperial Counselor Fan Yanshou, hearing of his ability, took him on staff, nominated him aide to the chamberlain of ceremonials, and Yong repeatedly memorialized on policy.

sentences.zh: 建始三年冬，日食、地震同日俱發，詔舉方正直言極諫之士，太常陽城侯劉慶忌舉永待詔公車。

translations.literal: In winter of the third year of Jianshi, solar eclipse and earthquake on the same day both broke out; an edict ordered recommendation of men of square integrity who spoke directly and remonstrated to the utmost; Chamberlain Marquis of Yangcheng Liu Qingji recommended Yong to await edicts at the public carriage.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Jianshi, winter brought a solar eclipse and an earthquake on the same day. The throne called for blunt critics; Liu Qingji, Marquis of Yangcheng and chamberlain of ceremonials, nominated Gu Yong to the waiting roster at the public carriage office.

sentences.zh: 對曰：

translations.literal: He replied, saying:
translations.idiomatic: He answered:

sentences.zh: 陛下踐至尊之祚為天下主，奉帝王之職以統群生，方內之治亂，在陛下所執。

translations.literal: Your Majesty treads the supreme fortune's seat as lord of the realm, bears the emperor-king's duty to rule the multitude; order or disorder within the square, lies in what Your Majesty grasps.
translations.idiomatic: You occupy the throne and bear the charge of shepherding every life; whether the realm stands firm or falters rests in your hands alone.

sentences.zh: 誠留意於正身，勉強於力行，損燕私之閒以勞天下，放去淫溺之樂，罷歸倡優之笑，絕卻不享之義，慎節游田之虞，起居有常，循禮而動，躬親政事，致行無倦，安服若性。

translations.literal: If truly you set mind on rectifying the person, strive in forceful conduct, lessen private banquet leisure to toil for the realm, release and cast away dissolving indulgence's pleasures, dismiss and return entertainers' laughter, cut off and reject not-sacrificing's propriety, carefully limit hunting's hazards, rising and resting have constancy, move following ritual, personally attend to government affairs, carry conduct without weariness, peaceful submission as if nature.
translations.idiomatic: Set your heart on self-correction and resolute action: trim private revelry to labor for the state, cast off sensual excess, send singers and jesters home, end neglect of sacrifice, curb the dangers of the hunt, keep regular hours, act only by ritual, take the reins of government yourself, and never tire of right conduct until it becomes second nature.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「繼自今嗣王，其毋淫於酒，毋逸於游田，惟正之共。」

translations.literal: The classic says: "From now on, succeeding kings—do not be dissolute in wine, do not be idle in roaming and hunting; only correct conduct offer together."
translations.idiomatic: The canon warns future kings: "No drunkenness, no heedless sport in the field—only upright service."

sentences.zh: 未有身治正而臣下邪者也。

translations.literal: There has never been one whose person was governed upright yet whose subordinates were wicked.
translations.idiomatic: When the ruler's person is straight, the ministers do not go crooked.

sentences.zh: 夫妻之際，王事綱紀，安危之機，聖王所致慎也。

translations.literal: The threshold between husband and wife is the kingly affair's guideline and cord; the pivot of safety and danger—what sagely kings have been most careful about.
translations.idiomatic: The bond between sovereign and consort is the backbone of kingship and the hinge of fortune; the sage kings weighed it with utmost care.

sentences.zh: 昔舜飭正二女，以崇至德；

translations.literal: Anciently Shun ordered and rectified the two daughters, thereby to exalt utmost virtue;
translations.idiomatic: Shun disciplined his two royal brides to perfect his virtue;

sentences.zh: 楚莊忍絕丹姬，以成伯功；

translations.literal: King Zhuang of Chu endured cutting off Lady Dan, thereby to complete hegemon's merit;
translations.idiomatic: King Zhuang of Chu renounced his favorite Dan Ji to win hegemony;

sentences.zh: 幽王惑於褒姒，周德降亡；

translations.literal: King You was deluded by Bao Si, and Zhou's virtue sank to extinction;
translations.idiomatic: King You drowned in Bao Si and the Zhou mandate collapsed;

sentences.zh: 魯桓脅於齊女，社稷以傾。

translations.literal: Duke Huan of Lu was coerced by the Qi woman, and altars of soil and grain thereby overturned.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan of Lu let a princess of Qi overawe him and lost his state.

sentences.zh: 誠修後宮之政，明尊卑之序，貴者不得嫉妨專龐，以絕驕嫚之端，抑褒、閻之亂，賤者咸得秩進，各得厥職，以廣繼嗣之統，息《白華》之怨，後宮親屬，饒之以財，勿與政事，以遠皇父之類，損妻黨之權，未有閨門治而天下亂者也。

translations.literal: If truly you repair inner-palace government, clarify honored and humble's order, the honored may not envy, obstruct, monopolize, or swell in favor—thereby to cut off arrogance and slights' beginnings, suppress Bao and Yan's disorder, the lowly all obtain orderly advance, each obtains his duty, thereby to broaden heir-bearing's thread, rest the "White Flowers" ode's resentment; inner-palace kin, enrich them with wealth, do not give them government affairs, thereby to distance royal-father types, lessen wife faction's power—there has never been inner-chamber governed yet realm disordered.
translations.idiomatic: Reform the harem: fix precedence so favorites cannot monopolize favor or breed arrogance; check the chaos of Bao Si and Zhao Feiyan; let humble consorts rise in due order, each to her station, to widen the chance of heirs and still the jealousies sung in the "White Flowers"; enrich in-laws with gold but bar them from office like the royal fathers of old, and clip the power of consort clans—when the inner quarters are ordered, the realm does not fall into chaos.

sentences.zh: 治遠自近始，習善在左右。

translations.literal: Governing what is far begins from what is near; practicing good lies in those at left and right.
translations.idiomatic: Rule the distant by ordering what is near; cultivate virtue first among those at your elbow.

sentences.zh: 昔龍管納言，而帝命惟允；

translations.literal: Anciently Long managed intake of words, and the emperor's commands were trustworthy;
translations.idiomatic: When Long held the office of remonstrance, the sovereign's word was true;

sentences.zh: 四輔既備，成王靡有過事。

translations.literal: When the four assistants were complete, King Cheng had no faulty affairs.
translations.idiomatic: with the four counselors in place, the young King Cheng never stumbled.

sentences.zh: 誠敕正左右齊栗之臣，戴金貂之飾、執常伯之職者，皆使學先王之道，知君臣之義，濟濟謹孚，無敖戲驕恣之地，則左右肅艾，群僚仰法，化流四方。

translations.literal: If truly you order and rectify the left and right ministers of equal awe, those who wear gold marten finery and hold the regular elder's duties—make them all study former kings' Way, know lord and minister's righteousness, in dense array be careful and sincere, have no place for arrogance, sport, pride, and self-will—then left and right are solemn and ordered, the throng of officials look up to the model, and transformation flows to the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Command every awe-struck attendant who bears the golden marten and holds a chief counselor's post to study the Way of the ancients and the duty between ruler and minister; let them stand grave and trustworthy, with no room for swagger or caprice—then your entourage grows disciplined, the bureaucracy takes its cue, and civilizing influence spreads to the four quarters.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「亦惟先正克左右。」

translations.literal: The classic says: "Also it is the former rectifiers who aid left and right."
translations.idiomatic: The text says, "It is upright men who steady those at your side."

sentences.zh: 未有左右正而百官枉者也。

translations.literal: There has never been left and right upright yet the hundred officials crooked.
translations.idiomatic: When those at your side are straight, the bureaucracy does not go awry.

sentences.zh: 治天下者尊賢考功則治，簡賢違功則亂。

translations.literal: Those who govern the realm, if they honor the worthy and examine merit, are ordered; if they neglect the worthy and depart from merit, are disordered.
translations.idiomatic: Honor talent and judge by results, and the realm is governed; slight the worthy and ignore performance, and it slides into chaos.

sentences.zh: 誠審思治人之術，歡樂得賢之福，論材選士，必試於職，明度量以程能，考功實以定德，無用比周之虛譽，毋聽浸潤之譖訴，則抱功修職之吏無蔽傷之憂，比周邪偽之徒不得即工，小人日銷，俊艾日隆。

translations.literal: If truly you carefully ponder the art of governing people, rejoice in obtaining the worthy's blessing, discuss material and select gentlemen, must try them in office, clarify measures and degrees to gauge ability, examine merit's substance to fix virtue, do not use clique's empty fame, do not heed soaking slander and complaints—then clerks who embrace merit and cultivate duty have no worry of concealment and injury; cliqueing wicked and false persons cannot attain to work; petty men daily wane, the fine daily rise.
translations.idiomatic: Think hard how to govern men, delight in winning true talent, match candidates to posts, and test them in office; use clear standards to measure ability and real deeds to judge character; spurn hollow praise from cabals and ignore drip-by-drip slander—then diligent officials need not fear being ruined in secret, sycophants cannot worm into power, the mean fade away, and the worthy rise.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「三載考績，三考黜陟幽明。」

translations.literal: The classic says: "Every three years examine merit; thrice examining, demote and promote the dim and the bright."
translations.idiomatic: The canon runs: "Review performance every three years; after three reviews, promote the bright and dismiss the dim."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「九德咸事，俊艾在官。」

translations.literal: It also says: "The nine virtues all in service, the fine and great are in office."
translations.idiomatic: It adds, "When the nine virtues are all at work, the finest men fill the government."

sentences.zh: 未有功賞得於前眾賢佈於官而不治者也。

translations.literal: There has never been merit and reward obtained beforehand yet many worthies spread through office yet not ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Reward merit first, spread able men through the bureaucracy, and the state cannot fail to be well governed.

sentences.zh: 堯遭洪水之災，天下分絕為十二州，制遠之道微而無乖畔之難者，德厚恩深，無怨於下也。

translations.literal: Yao suffered the flood's calamity; the realm was split and cut off into twelve provinces; the way of controlling what was far grew slight yet there was no rebellious defiance's difficulty—virtue was thick and grace deep, no resentment below.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yao the world was shattered by flood and carved into twelve regions; control of the periphery grew weak, yet the people did not revolt—such was the depth of his virtue that none nursed a grudge.

sentences.zh: 秦居平土，一夫大呼而海內崩析者，刑罰深酷，吏行殘賊也。

translations.literal: Qin dwelt on level earth; one man gave a great shout and within the seas collapsed and split—punishments were deep and cruel, officials practiced cruelty and banditry.
translations.idiomatic: Qin ruled level ground, yet a single cry brought the empire down—because its laws were savage and its clerks predatory.

sentences.zh: 夫違天害德，為上取怨於下，莫甚乎殘賊之吏。

translations.literal: As for violating Heaven and harming virtue, for the ruler to fetch resentment from below—nothing is worse than cruel and bandit officials.
translations.idiomatic: Nothing turns Heaven and the people against the throne faster than brutal magistrates.

sentences.zh: 誠放退殘賊酷暴之吏錮廢勿用，益選溫良上德之士以親萬勝，平刑釋冤以理民命，務省繇役，毋奪民時，薄收賦稅，毋殫民財，使天下黎元咸安家樂業，不苦逾時之役，不患苛暴之政，不疾酷烈之吏，雖有唐堯之大災，民無離上之心。

translations.literal: If truly you dismiss and retire cruel, bandit, harsh, and violent clerks, bar and cast aside without use, increase selection of warm, good, and high-virtue gentlemen to draw near the myriad victories, level punishments and release wrongs to order the people's lives, strive to lessen corvée labor, do not seize the people's seasons, lightly collect levies and taxes, do not exhaust the people's wealth, causing all the black-haired of the realm to rest their homes and rejoice in their occupations, not to suffer beyond-seasons labor, not to worry about harsh violent government, not to hate cruel fierce clerks—though there were Tang Yao's great calamity, the people would have no heart to leave the ruler above.
translations.idiomatic: Dismiss every cruel official, bar them forever from office, promote gentle men of high character to care for the myriad households, right wrongs and ease sentences, cut corvée so as not to steal the farming seasons, lighten taxes so as not to drain the purse—then the people will live in peace, unvexed by endless labor or harsh rule or pitiless clerks, and even a flood like Yao's would not shake their loyalty.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「懷保小人，惠於鰥寡。」

translations.literal: The classic says: "Cherish and protect the petty people; show grace to widowers and widows."
translations.idiomatic: The text bids the ruler cherish the common folk and show special kindness to widows and widowers.

sentences.zh: 未有德厚吏良而民畔者也。

translations.literal: There has never been virtue thick and clerks good yet the people rebel.
translations.idiomatic: When the sovereign is benevolent and his magistrates honest, the people do not rise in revolt.

sentences.zh: 臣聞災異，皇天所以譴告人君過失，猶嚴父之明誡。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that portents are how August Heaven censures and announces the human ruler's faults—like a stern father's clear warning.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that omens are Heaven's way of rebuking a sovereign's faults, as a strict father warns a son.

sentences.zh: 畏懼敬改，則禍銷福降；

translations.literal: If in fear and dread one respectfully reforms, then calamity melts and blessing descends;
translations.idiomatic: Dread them and mend your ways, and disaster yields to blessing;

sentences.zh: 忽然簡易，則咎罰不除。

translations.literal: if suddenly one is careless and easy, then blame and punishment are not removed.
translations.idiomatic: treat them lightly, and guilt and punishment never lift.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「饗用五福，畏用六極。」

translations.literal: The classic says: "With the five blessings feast; with the six extremes fear."
translations.idiomatic: The text says Heaven rewards with five blessings and chastises with six extremes.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「六沴作見，若不共禦，六罰既侵，六極其下。」

translations.literal: The tradition says: "When the six excesses appear as signs, if you do not jointly ward them off, the six punishments already invade, the six extremes descend below."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary adds that when the six baleful signs appear, failure to meet them together brings the six punishments and the six extremes upon the realm.

sentences.zh: 今三年之間，災異鋒起，小大畢具，所行不享上帝，上帝不豫，炳然甚著。

translations.literal: Now within three years, portents have risen like spear points, small and large all complete; what is practiced does not sacrifice to High God; High God is not pleased—brilliantly it is very manifest.
translations.idiomatic: In three years omens have flared without cease, great and small alike; your conduct has failed to honor High Heaven, and Heaven's displeasure blazes plain for all to see.

sentences.zh: 不求之身，無所改正，疏舉廣謀，又不用其言，是循不享之跡，無謝過之實也，天責愈深。

translations.literal: If you do not seek it in your person, there is nowhere to correct and reform; memorials raise broad counsel, yet again you do not use their words—this is following the track of not sacrificing, without the substance of apologizing for fault; Heaven's blame grows deeper.
translations.idiomatic: Without searching your own heart there can be no reform; though counsel pours in from every side, you ignore it—thus you persist in refusing Heaven's sacrifice and never truly repent, and Heaven's rebuke deepens.

sentences.zh: 此五者，王事之綱紀。

translations.literal: These five are the kingly affair's guideline and cord.
translations.idiomatic: These five points are the pillars of kingship.

sentences.zh: 南面之急務，唯陛下留神。

translations.literal: They are the south-facing ruler's urgent tasks—only may Your Majesty set mind on them.
translations.idiomatic: They are the urgent business of the throne; I beg you to weigh them with care.

sentences.zh: 對奏，天子異焉，特召見永。

translations.literal: When the reply was memorialized, the Son of Heaven marveled at it and specially summoned and saw Yong.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was struck by the memorial and summoned Gu Yong for a private audience.

sentences.zh: 其夏，皆令諸方正對策，語在《杜欽傳》。

translations.literal: That summer, all were ordered to let the various square-integrity men answer policy questions—the account is in the "Biography of Du Qin".
translations.idiomatic: That summer every candidate of square integrity was examined in debate; the details appear in Du Qin's biography.

sentences.zh: 永對畢，因曰：「臣前幸得條對災異之效，禍亂所極，言關於聖聰。

translations.literal: When Yong had finished answering, he thereupon said: "Your servant formerly was fortunate to itemize reply on portents' effects, where calamity and disorder reach extreme, words touching sagely hearing.
translations.idiomatic: When he had finished, Yong added: "I was once allowed to set forth how portents foretell disaster—matters that touched your sacred ear.

sentences.zh: 書陳於前，陛下委棄不納，而更使方正對策，背可懼之大異，問不急之常論，廢承天之至言，角無用之虛文，欲末殺災異，滿讕誣天，是故皇天勃然發怒，甲己之間暴風三溱，拔樹折木，此天至明不可欺之效也。」

translations.literal: The writing was displayed before you, yet Your Majesty cast it aside and did not accept it, and instead again made square-integrity men answer policy—turning your back on greatly to be feared prodigies, asking unurgent common discourse, discarding Heaven-receiving utmost words, contending useless empty texts, wishing to crush and kill portents, filling deceit and slandering Heaven—therefore August Heaven suddenly blazed in anger; between jia and ji three times violent wind rose, uprooting trees and snapping wood—this is Heaven's utmost clarity, the effect of what cannot be deceived."
translations.idiomatic: You set my memorial aside, then called for more policy essays—turning from awesome portents to pedantic trivia, spurning words that bear Heaven's charge for hollow rhetoric. You meant to explain away the omens and deceive Heaven; so Heaven answered with three great windstorms between the jia and ji days, tearing up trees—a sign that High Heaven cannot be mocked."

sentences.zh: 上特復問永，永對曰：「日食、地震，皇后、貴妾專寵所致。」

translations.literal: The emperor specially again questioned Yong; Yong replied, saying: "Solar eclipse and earthquake are caused by the empress and honored concubines' exclusive favor."
translations.idiomatic: Questioned again, Yong answered that the eclipse and quake came from the empress and favored consorts monopolizing the emperor's affection.

sentences.zh: 語在《五行志》。

translations.literal: The words are in the "Treatise on the Five Phases".
translations.idiomatic: The particulars are recorded in the Treatise on the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 是時，上初即位，謙讓委政元舅大將軍王鳳，議者多歸咎焉。

translations.literal: At this time the emperor had newly ascended the throne, modestly yielded and entrusted government to his eldest maternal uncle Grand General Wang Feng; many in discussion attributed blame there.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had just succeeded and had handed power to his uncle, Grand General Wang Feng; many blamed the omens on him.

sentences.zh: 永知鳳方見柄用，陰欲自托，乃復曰：

translations.literal: Yong knew Feng was then seen wielded in use; secretly he wished to attach himself; thereupon he again said:
translations.idiomatic: Knowing Feng was ascendant, Yong sought quietly to win his favor and added:

sentences.zh: 方今四夷賓服，皆為臣妾，北無薰粥冒頓之患，南無趙佗、呂嘉之難，三垂晏然，靡有兵革之警。

translations.literal: Now the four barbarians guest-submit, all are servants and concubines; north has no Xunyu Modun's trouble, south has no Zhao Tuo or Lu Jia's difficulty; the three margins are quiet, none have arms and armor's alarm.
translations.idiomatic: The barbarians are all tributary; the north holds no Xiongnu threat like Modun's day, the south no rebellion like Zhao Tuo or Lu Jia; the frontiers are calm and no call to arms sounds.

sentences.zh: 諸侯大者乃食數縣，漢吏制其權柄，不得有為，亡吳、楚、燕、梁之勢。

translations.literal: The greatest feudal lords eat only several counties; Han clerks control their power handles, they cannot accomplish deeds—there is no Wu, Chu, Yan, or Liang's strength.
translations.idiomatic: Even the largest feudal lords feed on a few counties only, while Han officials hold their levers of power—nowhere is there strength like old Wu, Chu, Yan, or Liang.

sentences.zh: 百官盤互，親疏相錯，骨肉大臣有申伯之忠，洞洞屬屬，小心畏忌，無重合、安陽、博陸之亂。

translations.literal: The hundred officials coil and cross; near and far mutually interlace; bone-and-flesh great ministers have Shen Bo's loyalty, in awe linked and linked, careful in fear and dread—there is no Chonge, Anyang, or Bolu disorder.
translations.idiomatic: The bureaucracy is a web of kin and stranger, yet your kinsmen-ministers show the loyalty of Shen Bo—solemn, cautious, nothing like the Chonge, Huo, or Han clique rebellions.

sentences.zh: 三者無毛髮之辜，不可歸咎諸舅。

translations.literal: In these three there is not hair's breadth of guilt; one cannot lay blame on the various uncles.
translations.idiomatic: On these three counts the maternal kin are blameless as a hair's weight.

sentences.zh: 及欲以政事過差丞相父子、中尚書宦官，檻塞大異，皆□說欺天者也。

translations.literal: As for wishing by government affairs' faults to shift blame to chancellor father and son, palace secretariat and eunuchs, caging and blocking great prodigies—all are deceptive talk that deceives Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: To blame the chancellor and his son, the inner secretariat, or eunuchs for administrative slips, or to fence off great portents with petty excuses, is to lie to Heaven.

sentences.zh: 竊恐陛下捨昭昭之白過，忽天地之明戒，聽暗昧之□說，歸咎乎無辜，倚異乎政事，重失天心，不可之大者也。

translations.literal: Your servant privately fears Your Majesty will cast away the bright plain faults, neglect Heaven and Earth's clear warnings, heed dark and dim deceptive talk, lay blame on the guiltless, rely on prodigies in government affairs, doubly lose Heaven's heart—this is of the greatest what cannot be.
translations.idiomatic: I fear you may ignore your own clear faults, spurn Heaven's plain warnings, heed shadowy slander, punish the innocent, read strange meanings into routine policy, and doubly forfeit the Mandate—that would be intolerable.

sentences.zh: 陛下即位，委任遵舊，未有過政。

translations.literal: Since Your Majesty ascended the throne, you have entrusted duties following precedent; there has been no faulty government.
translations.idiomatic: Since your accession you have followed established usage in appointments; you have not misgoverned.

sentences.zh: 元年正月，白氣較然起乎東方，至其四月，黃濁四塞，覆冒京師，申以大水，著以震蝕。

translations.literal: In the first year's first month, white vapor clearly rose in the east; reaching its fourth month, yellow turbidity blocked the four sides, covering and overspreading the capital; it was extended by great flood, made manifest by quake and eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: In your first year, first month, a white aura rose in the east; by the fourth month yellow murk choked the capital, followed by flood and by earthquake and eclipse—each omen matching the other.

sentences.zh: 各有占應，相為表裡，百官庶事無所歸倚，陛下獨不怪與？

translations.literal: Each has divination response, mutually outer and inner; the hundred officials and myriad affairs have nowhere to lean—does Your Majesty alone not find it strange?
translations.idiomatic: They answer one another as inner and outer signs; no official can tell where to stand—is this not strange to you alone?

sentences.zh: 白氣起東方，賤人將興之表也；

translations.literal: White vapor rising in the east is the sign of mean persons about to rise;
translations.idiomatic: White vapor in the east foretells the rise of commoners to power;

sentences.zh: 黃濁冒京師，王道微絕之應也。

translations.literal: yellow turbidity overspreading the capital is the response of the kingly way growing faint to cutting off.
translations.idiomatic: yellow haze over the capital answers to a royal Way grown faint to the breaking point.

sentences.zh: 夫賤人當起而京師道微，二者已丑。

translations.literal: Mean persons about to rise while the capital's way grows faint—the two things already are ugly together.
translations.idiomatic: Low-born power rising while the capital's virtue fails—the two omens compound an ill augury.

sentences.zh: 陛下誠深察愚臣之言，致懼天地之異，長思宗廟之計，改往反過，抗湛溺之意，解偏駁之愛，奮乾剛之威，平天覆之施，使列妾得人人更進，猶尚未足也，急復益納宜子婦人，毋擇好醜，毋避嘗字，毋論年齒。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty truly deeply examines your foolish servant's words, brings fear of Heaven and Earth's prodigies, long ponders the altars' plan, changes past and reverses faults, resists drowning indulgence's intent, loosens partial and uneven favor, rouses Qian-gang's awesomeness, levels Heaven-covering's bestowal, causing ranked concubines to obtain each in turn advance—even this is still not enough; urgently again increase and take in women fit to bear sons, do not choose beauty or ugliness, do not avoid taboo-homophone characters, do not discuss age.
translations.idiomatic: If you heed this counsel, dread Heaven's signs, think long on the ancestral shrines, mend past errors, shake off sensual obsession and lopsided favor, show the firmness of the sovereign's will, and spread your favor evenly so every consort may in turn attend you—even that would not suffice: you must urgently take in more women likely to bear sons, without regard to looks, taboo homonyms, or age.

sentences.zh: 推法言之，陛下得繼嗣於微賤之間，乃反為福。

translations.literal: Pushing by pattern to speak of it, Your Majesty obtaining heir in mean and low places is on the contrary blessing.
translations.idiomatic: By every principle of state, an heir born to a humble mother would prove a blessing.

sentences.zh: 得繼嗣而已，母非有賤也。

translations.literal: Once heir is obtained then stopping, the mother is not thereby base.
translations.idiomatic: Once there is an heir, the mother's low rank ceases to matter.

sentences.zh: 後宮女吏使令有直意者，廣求於微賤之間，以遇天所開右，慰釋皇太后之憂慍，解謝上帝之譴怒，則繼嗣蕃滋，災異訖息。

translations.literal: Among inner-palace women clerks and orderlies who have straight intent, broadly seek in mean and low places, thereby to meet what Heaven opens and aids, soothe and release the Grand Empress Dowager's worry and resentment, untie and apologize for High God's reproaching anger—then heirs will flourish and multiply, portents will end and cease.
translations.idiomatic: Send trustworthy women of the harem to search widely among humble families for matches Heaven favors, ease the empress dowager's anxiety, and appease divine anger—then heirs will multiply and the omens will cease.

sentences.zh: 陛下則不深察愚臣之言，忽於天地之戒，咎根不除，水雨之災，山石之異，將發不久；

translations.literal: If Your Majesty then does not deeply examine your foolish servant's words, neglects Heaven and Earth's warnings, the fault's root is not removed—water and rain calamities, mountain and stone prodigies will break out before long;
translations.idiomatic: If you ignore this counsel and slight Heaven's warning, the root of evil remains—flood and strange stones will soon follow;

sentences.zh: 發則災異已極，天變成形，臣雖欲捐身關策，不及事已。

translations.literal: when they break out then portents reach the utmost, Heaven's change takes form—though your servant wished to cast away body and present stratagem, it would not catch up with affairs already done.
translations.idiomatic: once they break, calamity will be complete and Heaven's shape fixed—though I would give my life to counsel you, it would be too late.

sentences.zh: 疏賤之臣，至敢直陳天意，斥譏帷幄之私，欲間離貴後、盛妾，自知忤心逆耳，必不免於湯鑊之誅。

translations.literal: A mean and low minister, reaching to dare straightly declare Heaven's intent, rebuke and ridicule curtain's privacy, wishing to divide and separate honored empress and flourishing concubines—himself knowing it offends the heart and reverses the ear, cannot avoid boiling cauldron execution.
translations.idiomatic: A humble subject who dares speak Heaven's mind aloud, who mocks what passes within the bed-curtains and would part the empress from a powerful favorite, knows his words grate and invite the cauldron.

sentences.zh: 此天保右漢家，使臣敢直言也。

translations.literal: This is Heaven protecting and aiding the Han house, causing your servant to dare speak straight.
translations.idiomatic: Only because Heaven guards the house of Han do I dare speak so plainly.

sentences.zh: 三上封事，然後得召；

translations.literal: Three times submitting sealed matters, then he was summoned;
translations.idiomatic: I had to submit three sealed memorials before I was summoned;

sentences.zh: 待詔一旬，然後得見。

translations.literal: awaiting edicts one ten-day week, then he was able to be seen.
translations.idiomatic: then waited ten days on the roster before I gained an audience.

sentences.zh: 夫由疏賤納至忠，甚苦；

translations.literal: As for by mean and low to bring in utmost loyalty—it is very bitter;
translations.idiomatic: For a nobody to win a hearing for utmost loyalty is bitter work;

sentences.zh: 由至尊聞天意，甚難。

translations.literal: for the most honored to hear Heaven's intent—it is very hard.
translations.idiomatic: for the Son of Heaven to heed Heaven's voice is harder still.

sentences.zh: 語不可露，願具書所言，因待中奏陛下，以示腹心大臣。

translations.literal: Words cannot be exposed; I wish fully to write what is said, thereby through attendant-in-waiting memorialize Your Majesty, to show heart-and-belly great ministers.
translations.idiomatic: These things cannot be spoken in the open: let me set them down in writing for a chamber attendant to present to you and to your closest ministers.

sentences.zh: 腹心大臣以為非天意，臣當伏妄言之誅；

translations.literal: If heart-and-belly great ministers deem it not Heaven's intent, your servant ought to submit to execution for wild speech;
translations.idiomatic: If those ministers judge my words false to Heaven's mind, I will accept death for reckless speech;

sentences.zh: 即以為誠天意也，奈何忘國家大本，背天意而從欲！

translations.literal: if they deem it truly Heaven's intent, how can you forget the state's great root, turn your back on Heaven's intent and follow desire!
translations.idiomatic: if they agree it is Heaven's truth, how can you ignore the foundation of the dynasty and choose desire over the Mandate!

sentences.zh: 唯陛下省察熟念，厚為宗廟計。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty examine and ponder maturely, thickly plan for the ancestral shrines.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to reflect long and hard for the sake of the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 時，對者數十人，永與杜欽為上第焉。

translations.literal: At the time, those who answered were several tens of persons; Yong together with Du Qin were of the upper rank.
translations.idiomatic: Of the dozens who responded, Gu Yong and Du Qin ranked highest.

sentences.zh: 上皆以其書示後宮。

translations.literal: The emperor all took his writings and showed the inner palace.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had his memorials circulated in the harem.

sentences.zh: 後上嘗賜許皇后書，采永言以責之，語在《外戚傳》。

translations.literal: Afterward the emperor once bestowed a letter on Empress Xu, adopting Yong's words to reproach her—the account is in the "Treatise on the Outer Kin".
translations.idiomatic: Later the emperor sent Empress Xu a letter borrowing Yong's phrases to chastise her, as told among the consort kin biographies.

sentences.zh: 永既陰為大將軍鳳說矣，能實最高，由是擢為光祿大夫。

translations.literal: Yong having secretly spoken for Grand General Feng, his substance of ability was highest; thereupon he was promoted to grand counselor of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Having quietly pleaded Wang Feng's cause, Yong was judged the ablest candidate and was raised to grand counselor of the palace.

sentences.zh: 永奏書謝鳳曰：「永斗筲之材，質薄學朽，無一日之雅，左右之介，將軍說其狂言，擢之皁衣之吏，廁之爭臣之末，不聽浸潤之譖，不食膚受之訴，雖齊桓、晉文用士篤密，察父哲兄覆育子弟，誠無以加！

translations.literal: Yong submitted a memorial thanking Feng, saying: "Yong is dipper-and-scoop stuff, substance thin and learning rotten, without one day's refinement, without go-between at left and right; the general was pleased with his wild words, raised him to black-robed clerk, placed him in remonstrating ministers' end, did not listen to soaking slander, did not swallow skin-deep complaints—even Duke Huan of Qi and Lord Wen of Jin in employing gentlemen were thick and close, wise father and wise elder brother covering and nurturing sons and younger brothers—in truth nothing could be added!"
translations.idiomatic: He wrote Wang Feng: "I am a trifling talent, shallow and ill-read, a stranger to you without introduction, yet you welcomed my blunt words, raised me from a petty clerk to the ranks of remonstrators, and refused both whispered slander and shallow complaint. Not even Duke Huan of Qi or Lord Wen of Jin treated their men with such care—no fond father or elder brother could do more."

sentences.zh: 昔豫子吞炭壞形以奉見異，齊客隕首公門以報恩施，知氏、孟嘗猶有死士，何況將軍之門！」

translations.literal: Anciently Yuzi swallowed charcoal and ruined form to serve and show strangeness; a Qi guest dashed out brains at the ducal gate to repay grace shown; the Zhi clan and Lord Mengchang still had death-sworn men—how much more the general's gate!"
translations.idiomatic: Yurang disfigured himself with charcoal for his lord; a Qi retainer dashed out his brains at the gate to repay a kindness. Even the houses of Zhi Bo and Lord Mengchang kept men willing to die—how much more may your own gate command such loyalty!"

sentences.zh: 鳳遂厚之。

translations.literal: Feng thereupon treated him generously.
translations.idiomatic: Feng thereafter treated him with great favor.

sentences.zh: 永遠為郡吏，恐為音所危，病滿三月免。

translations.literal: Yong was transferred to be commandery clerk; fearing to be endangered by Yin, he feigned illness full three months and was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: When Yong was posted as a commandery clerk, he feared Wang Yin would harm him and pleaded illness for three months until he was released.

sentences.zh: 音奏請永補營軍司馬，永數謝罪自陳，得轉為長史。

translations.literal: Yin memorialized requesting Yong fill camp army marshal; Yong several times apologized for guilt and stated himself, was able to be transferred to chief clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Yin asked to appoint him camp marshal; Yong repeatedly begged off in self-defense and was instead made chief clerk.

sentences.zh: 音用從舅越親輔政，威權損於鳳時，永復說音曰：「將軍覆上將之位，食豪腴之都，任周、召之職，擁天下之樞，可謂富貴之極，人臣無二，天下之責四面至矣，將何以居之？

translations.literal: Yin by maternal uncle's kin overstepping nearness aided government; awe and power were less than in Feng's time; Yong again exhorted Yin, saying: "General, you cover the highest general's position, eat from rich and fat capitals, bear the Duke of Zhou and Duke of Shao's duties, grasp the realm's pivot—it may be called wealth and honor's utmost, among ministers none second; the realm's blame comes from all four sides—how will you dwell in it?"
translations.idiomatic: When Yin came to power through a maternal uncle and his authority fell short of Wang Feng's, Yong urged him: "You hold a marshal's rank, feed on the richest commanderies, bear the burden of the Zhou dukes, and turn the empire's hinge—you have reached the peak of wealth and power, yet blame now converges from every quarter. How will you bear it?"

sentences.zh: 宜夙夜孳孳，執伊尹之強德，以守職匡上，誅惡不避親愛，舉善不避仇讎，以章至公，立信四方。

translations.literal: You ought morning and night be diligent, grasp Yi Yin's strong virtue, thereby to guard duty and correct the ruler, punish wickedness without shrinking from kin and loved ones, raise good without shrinking from foes and enemies, thereby to display utmost fairness, establish trust in the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Labor day and night in the stern virtue of Yi Yin: keep your charge, aid the throne, punish evil without sparing kin, promote good without shunning enemies—only thus can you show perfect fairness and win trust across the realm.

sentences.zh: 篤行三者，乃可以長堪重任，久享盛寵。

translations.literal: Earnestly practice these three, then you can long bear heavy charge, long enjoy flourishing favor.
translations.idiomatic: Faithfully do these three, and you may long bear heavy office and long keep supreme favor.

sentences.zh: 太白出西方六十日，法當參天，今已過期，尚在桑榆之間，質弱而行遲，形小而光微。

translations.literal: Grand White appeared in the west sixty days; by rule it ought to join Heaven; now it has already passed the term, still is between mulberry and elm—substance weak and motion slow, form small and light faint.
translations.idiomatic: Venus rose in the west for sixty days—by rule it should have climbed the zenith, yet it lingers low in the sky, faint and slow, small and dim: a weakened star.

sentences.zh: 熒惑角怒明大，逆行守尾。

translations.literal: Mars' horns angry, bright and large; retrograde, guarding the Tail.
translations.idiomatic: Mars blazes angry and bright, retrograde, lodged in the Tail constellation.

sentences.zh: 其逆，常也；

translations.literal: Its retrograde motion is constant;
translations.idiomatic: Retrograde motion is common enough;

sentences.zh: 守尾，變也。

translations.literal: guarding the Tail is change.
translations.idiomatic: to halt at the Tail is the omen of crisis.

sentences.zh: 意豈將軍忘湛漸之義，委曲從順，所執不強，不廣用士，尚有好惡之忌，蕩蕩之德未純，方與將相大臣乖離之萌也？

translations.literal: Could it be the general has forgotten steep soaking's meaning, bends and yields compliance, what is grasped is not strong, does not broadly employ gentlemen, still has love-hate's taboos, vast vast virtue not pure—just with generals and ministers great ministers the sprout of estrangement?
translations.idiomatic: Does it mean you have forgotten how power seeps like water—yielding everywhere, holding your course weakly, failing to employ men broadly, still nursing likes and dislikes—so that your broad virtue is not pure and the first rift opens between you and the great ministers of state?

sentences.zh: 何故始襲司馬之號，俄而金火並有此變？

translations.literal: Why reason: you first inherited marshal's title, and suddenly metal and fire together had this change?
translations.idiomatic: Why, the moment you took the title of marshal, should Venus and Mars together show such a change?

sentences.zh: 上天至明，不虛見異，唯將軍畏之慎之，深思其故，改求其路，以享天意。」

translations.literal: High Heaven is utmost clear; it does not vainly show prodigies—only, general, fear them and be careful, deeply ponder their cause, change and seek their road, thereby to enjoy Heaven's intent."
translations.idiomatic: High Heaven does not warn without cause. Dread these signs, search your heart, mend your course, and answer Heaven's will."

sentences.zh: 音猶不平，薦永為護菀使者。

translations.literal: Yin still was not appeased; he recommended Yong as commissioner for the imperial park.
translations.idiomatic: Yin, still resentful, had Yong appointed commissioner of the imperial hunting park.

sentences.zh: 臣聞王天下有國家者，患在上有危亡之事，而危亡之言不得上聞；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that for one who kings the realm and possesses the state, the worry lies above when there are peril and extinction matters, yet peril and extinction words cannot be heard above.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that the true peril for a sovereign is not danger itself but that warnings of danger never reach the throne.

sentences.zh: 如使危亡之言輒上聞，則商、周不易姓而迭興，三正不變改而更用。

translations.literal: If peril and extinction words were always heard above, then Shang and Zhou would not have changed surname yet risen in turn, the Three Rectifiers would not have altered and been used anew.
translations.idiomatic: Had every warning of ruin reached the throne, the Shang and Zhou would not have fallen in succession, nor would the Three Ages of calendar have turned over as they did.

sentences.zh: 夏、商之將亡也，行道之人皆知之，晏然自以若天有日莫能危，是故惡日廣而不自知，大命傾而不寤。

translations.literal: When Xia and Shang were about to perish, travelers on the road all knew it; yet they were at ease, deeming themselves as if Heaven had a sun none could endanger—therefore wickedness daily spread yet they did not know themselves, the great mandate tilted yet they did not wake.
translations.idiomatic: As Xia and Shang neared collapse, common gossip knew the truth, yet the courts lounged as if the sun would never set—evil spread unseen until the Mandate broke and they still did not stir.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「危者有其安者也，亡者保其存者也。」

translations.literal: The "Changes" says: "The endangered has his peace; the doomed preserves his existence."
translations.idiomatic: The "Changes" teaches: "He who senses danger keeps his peace; he who knows doom may yet preserve life."

sentences.zh: 陛下誠垂寬明之聽，無忌諱之誅，使芻蕘之臣得盡所聞於前，不懼於後患，直言之路開，則四方眾賢不遠千里，輻湊陳忠，群臣之上願，社稷之長福也。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty truly extends a broad clear ear, with no taboo executions, letting fodder-cutting ministers exhaust what they have heard before you without fear of later disaster, the path of straight speech opens—then worthies of the four quarters will not shrink from a thousand leagues, hub-like converge to display loyalty: it is the throng of ministers' highest wish and the altars' lasting blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Grant a candid hearing, punish no man for plain speech, and let the humblest clerk speak his mind without fear—then worthies will flock from every quarter, and that is both the ministers' dearest hope and the lasting good of the altars.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「在中饋，無攸遂」，言婦人不得與事也。

translations.literal: The "Changes" says: "In charge of the inner kettle, nowhere to succeed"—it means women may not join in affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The "Changes" says a woman's place is "the inner kettle"—she must not meddle in state business.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：「懿厥哲婦，為梟為鴟」；

translations.literal: The "Odes" says: "Ah that clever woman—owl and kite";
translations.idiomatic: The "Odes" cries: "That clever woman—owl and kite";

sentences.zh: 「匪降自天，生自婦人」。

translations.literal: "It did not descend from Heaven—it was born of women."
translations.idiomatic: "It did not fall from Heaven—it rose from the women's quarters."

sentences.zh: 建始、河平之際，許、班之貴，頃動前朝，熏灼四方，賞賜無量，空虛內臧，女寵至極，不可上矣；

translations.literal: At the juncture of Jianshi and Heping, the eminence of Xu and Ban in a moment shook the previous court, scorched the four directions, rewards without measure emptied the inner treasury; feminine favor reached the extreme, could not rise higher;
translations.idiomatic: Under Jianshi and Heping the houses of Xu and Ban dazzled the court, burned through the empire, poured out limitless gifts until the privy purse ran dry—feminine favor had climbed as high as it could;

sentences.zh: 今之後起，天所不饗，什倍於前。

translations.literal: Those who rise after them, what Heaven does not feast, are tenfold before.
translations.idiomatic: today's favorites, whom Heaven does not bless, outdo them tenfold.

sentences.zh: 廢先帝法度，聽用其言，官秩不當，縱釋王誅，驕其親屬，假之威權，從橫亂政，刺舉之吏，莫敢奉憲。

translations.literal: They cast aside the former emperor's statutes, heed their words, ranks are improper, they loosen and release royal punishments, arrogant with kinsmen, lend them awe and power, recklessly disorder government—investigating officers none dare uphold the statutes.
translations.idiomatic: They scrap the late emperor's laws, obey their whisperers, muddle official rank, waive capital crimes, puff up their kin and lend them power, and throw the government into chaos while no inspector dares enforce the code.

sentences.zh: 又以掖庭獄大為亂阱，榜棰□於砲格，絕滅人命，主為趙、李報德復怨，反除白罪，建治正吏，多系無辜，掠立迫恐，至為人起責，分利受謝。

translations.literal: Moreover they made the harem prison a great chaos pit; cudgels □ upon paoge, cutting off human lives; masters work Zhao and Li repaying grace and revenge, reverse clearing petty guilt, building cases against upright officials, binding many innocent, torture to force terror, even fabricating debts to split profit and take thanks.
translations.idiomatic: They turned the harem jail into a torture pit worse than the paoge, traded lives for Zhao and Li family vendettas, framed the innocent, extorted confessions, and invented debts to pocket bribes.

sentences.zh: 生入死出者，不可勝數。

translations.literal: Those who entered alive and left dead cannot be counted.
translations.idiomatic: The tally of those who walked in living and came out dead is beyond counting.

sentences.zh: 是以日食再既，以昭其辜。

translations.literal: Therefore the solar eclipse twice reached totality, to display their guilt.
translations.idiomatic: Hence Heaven twice blotted out the sun to publish their guilt.

sentences.zh: 王者必先自絕，然後天絕之。

translations.literal: The true king must first cut himself off, then Heaven cuts him off.
translations.idiomatic: A king must first forfeit the Way himself before Heaven forfeits him.

sentences.zh: 陛下棄萬乘之至貴，樂家人之賤事，厭高美之尊號，好匹夫之卑字，崇聚□輕無義小人以為私客，數離深宮之固，挺身晨夜，與群小相隨，烏集雜會，飲醉吏民之家，亂服共坐，流面媟嫚，混淆無別，閔免遁樂，晝夜在路。

translations.literal: Your Majesty casts away the ten-thousand chariots' supreme nobility, delights in household men's low matters, tires of the high and beautiful honor title, loves a commoner's low byname, gathers □ worthless faithless petty men as private guests, often leaves the deep palace's security, thrusts your body morning and night, following crowds of petty men, crows flock in mixed feasts, drunk in clerk and commoner homes, disorderly dress sitting together, faces smeared in lewdness, mixed without distinction, careless ease and roaming pleasure, day and night on the road.
translations.idiomatic: You abandon imperial dignity for the pleasures of the street, spurn your rightful style for a low nickname, pack your retinue with riffraff, slip the palace night and day, carouse in commoners' houses, sit in disheveled company, smear your face in debauch, and spend your nights on the road like a vagabond.

sentences.zh: 典門戶奉宿衛之臣執干戈而守空宮，公卿百僚不知陛下所在，積數年矣。

translations.literal: The ministers who keep gates and attend night watch hold halberds guarding empty palaces; dukes and ministers and the hundred ranks do not know where Your Majesty is—it has piled up for several years.
translations.idiomatic: Your guards stand at empty gates while the high ministers have no idea where you are—and this has gone on for years.

sentences.zh: 王者以民為基，民以財為本，財竭則下畔，下畔則下亡。

translations.literal: The true king takes the people as foundation; the people take wealth as root; when wealth is exhausted those below rebel; when those below rebel those below perish.
translations.idiomatic: The throne rests on the people, the people on their livelihood; drain their wealth and they revolt; revolt ends the dynasty.

sentences.zh: 是以明王愛養基本，不敢窮極，使民如承大祭。

translations.literal: Therefore the enlightened king cherishes and nurtures the root, dares not exhaust it to the limit, causing the people as if undertaking the great sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: The wise king husbands the people's strength as if preparing the grand sacrifice—never squeezing them dry.

sentences.zh: 今陛下輕奪民財，不愛民力，聽邪臣之計，去高敞初陵，捐十年功緒，改作昌陵，反天地之性，因下為高，積土為山，發徒起邑，並治宮館，大興繇役，重增賦斂，征發如雨，役百乾溪，費疑驪山，靡敝天下，五年不成而後反故。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty lightly seizes the people's wealth, does not cherish the people's strength, listens to wicked ministers' plans, abandons the high and broad first tomb, casts aside ten years' work, rebuilds Changling, contrary to Heaven and Earth's nature, making low into high, piling earth into mountains, dispatching laborers and raising towns, together building palaces and lodges, greatly raising corvée, heavily increasing levies and collections, conscriptions like rain, corvée a hundredfold Qianxi, expense rivaling Lishan, wearing out the realm—five years without completion then returned to the old site.
translations.idiomatic: You heed evil counsel, abandon the first mausoleum site, waste a decade of labor to raise Changling against nature—piling hills, dragging conscripts from town to town, throwing up palaces, doubling taxes, drafting men like rain until the burden recalls King Chu's Qianxi or the First Emperor's Lishan—exhausting the empire, and after five fruitless years you fell back to the old plan.

sentences.zh: 又廣盱營表，發人塚墓，斷截骸骨，暴揚屍柩，百姓財竭力盡，愁恨感天，災異屢降，饑饉仍臻。

translations.literal: Moreover you broadly gaze and camp the markers, dig up people's tombs, cut off bones, expose coffins; the hundred surnames' wealth is spent and strength gone, sorrow and hate touch Heaven, portents repeatedly descend, famine comes again and again.
translations.idiomatic: You widen the boundary, break open graves, scatter bones and coffins in the sun until the people are beggared and heartsick—Heaven answers with omens and repeated famine.

sentences.zh: 流散冗食，餧死於道，以百萬數。

translations.literal: Wandering scattered redundant eaters, starving to death on the road—by the hundred thousand count.
translations.idiomatic: Refugees starve by the hundred thousand along the roads.

sentences.zh: 公家無一年之畜，百姓無旬日之儲，上下俱匱，無以相救。

translations.literal: The public house has not one year's store; the hundred surnames lack ten days' reserve; above and below both are empty, nothing with which mutually to save.
translations.idiomatic: The treasury holds less than a year's grain, the common folk less than ten days' food—court and country are broke together.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「殷監不遠，在夏後之世。」

translations.literal: The "Odes" says: "Yin's mirror is not far—it is in Xia's generation."
translations.idiomatic: The "Odes" says Yin's lesson lies close at hand in the fall of Xia.

sentences.zh: 願陛下追觀夏、商、周、秦所以失之，以鏡考己行。

translations.literal: I wish Your Majesty to trace back and observe how Xia, Shang, Zhou, and Qin lost it, to mirror and examine your own conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Look back at how Xia, Shang, Zhou, and Qin fell, and hold that mirror to your own acts.

sentences.zh: 有不合者，臣當伏妄言之誅。

translations.literal: If there is what does not match, your servant ought to submit to execution for wild speech.
translations.idiomatic: If I am wrong, I will accept death for reckless counsel.

sentences.zh: 漢興九世，百九十餘載，繼體之主七，皆承天順道，遵先祖法度，或以中興，或以治安。

translations.literal: Han has risen nine generations, more than one hundred ninety years; inheriting-body lords seven—all received Heaven and followed the Way, obeyed former ancestors' statutes, some thereby mid-restoration, some thereby peace and order.
translations.idiomatic: For nine generations and nearly two hundred years Han has known seven succession emperors who kept Heaven's Way and ancestral law—some restoring order, some ruling in peace.

sentences.zh: 至於陛下，獨違道縱欲，輕身妄行，當盛壯之隆，無繼嗣之福，有危亡之憂，積失君道，不合天意，亦已多矣。

translations.literal: Coming to Your Majesty, alone you violate the Way and indulge desire, lightly body and rashly act; in the height of vigorous prime you lack heir-bearing blessing, have peril and extinction worry; faults as ruler have piled up, not matching Heaven's intent—already very many.
translations.idiomatic: You alone defy the Way and follow appetite; in your prime you lack an heir and court disaster; your lapses as sovereign have mounted until they no longer match Heaven's mind.

sentences.zh: 為人後嗣，守人功業，如此，豈不負哉！

translations.literal: Being another's heir, guarding another's achievement—like this, is it not betrayal!
translations.idiomatic: You are heir to a great house—can you betray that charge without shame?

sentences.zh: 方今社稷宗廟禍福安危之機在於陛下，陛下誠肯發明聖之德，昭然遠寤，畏此上天之威怒，深懼危亡之徵兆，蕩滌邪辟之惡志，厲精緻政，專心反道，絕群小之私客，免不正之詔除，悉罷北宮私奴車馬□出之具，克己復禮，毋二微行出飲之過，以防迫切之禍，深惟日食再既之意，抑損椒房玉堂之盛寵，毋聽後宮之請謁，除掖庭之亂獄，出砲格之陷阱，誅戮邪佞之臣及左右執左道以事上者以塞天下之望，且寢初陵之作，止諸繕治宮室，闕更減賦，盡休力役，存恤振救困乏之人以弭遠方，厲崇忠直，放退殘賊，無使素餐之吏久屍厚祿，以次貫行，固執無違，夙夜孳孳，屢省無怠，舊愆畢改，新德既章，纖介之邪不復載心，則赫赫大異庶幾可銷，天命去就庶幾可復，社稷宗廟庶幾可保。

translations.literal: Now the pivot of blessing and peril, safety and danger for altars and ancestral temple lies in Your Majesty; if Your Majesty truly will issue enlightened sage virtue, clearly wake from afar, fear this High Heaven's wrath, deeply dread peril and extinction's omens, wash away wicked and depraved evil intent, sharpen essence and order government, single-mindedly return to the Way, cut off petty men's private guests, dismiss improper edict appointments, entirely stop Northern Palace private slaves, carriages, horses, □ outing gear, restrain self and return to ritual, do not twice commit micro-journey drinking excess, to guard against pressing peril, deeply ponder the meaning of double solar eclipse, reduce pepper chamber and Jade Hall's flourishing favor, do not heed inner palace's requests and visits, remove harem's chaotic prisons, cast out paoge traps, execute and slaughter wicked flattering ministers and those at left and right who hold crooked ways to serve the ruler, to stop the realm's expectations; moreover halt first tomb's construction, stop all palace repair, reduce and lighten levies, wholly rest forced labor, preserve and relieve the exhausted to quiet the distant, promote loyalty and straightness, dismiss cruel bandits, do not let white-meal clerks long corpse fat salaries—in order carry through, firmly hold without wavering, morning and night diligent, often examine without slack, old faults all changed, new virtue already manifest, hair's-breadth evil no longer loaded on heart—then blazing great prodigies perhaps can melt, Heaven's mandate's going or staying perhaps can return, altars and temple perhaps can be preserved.
translations.idiomatic: The fate of the dynasty rests with you: rouse your virtue, dread Heaven's anger, scourge evil counsel, govern with vigor, banish your low companions and irregular appointments, strip the Northern Palace of its private carriages and outings, keep ritual and end secret drinking, read the lesson of the twin eclipses, curb the harem's power, reject palace pleas, empty the harem jails and torture pits, execute corrupt favorites and occultists who mislead you, stop the mausoleum works, end palace building, cut taxes and corvée, feed the starving, honor the loyal, sack the cruel, drive out idle officials who fatten on salary—hold to this without wavering, amend old faults and let new virtue show, and let no petty vice lodge in your heart. Then the great omens may fade, the Mandate may return, and the altars may stand.

sentences.zh: 唯陛下留神反覆，熟省臣言。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty set mind on turning back and forth, maturely examine your servant's words.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to turn these words over in your mind again and again.

sentences.zh: 臣幸得備邊部之吏，不知本朝失得，□言觸忌諱，罪當萬死。

translations.literal: Your servant is fortunate to fill a border department clerk, not knowing this court's gain and loss; □ words touch taboos, guilt deserves ten thousand deaths.
translations.idiomatic: I am only a frontier officer who does not know court secrets; if I have touched taboo, I deserve death ten thousand times.

sentences.zh: 成帝性寬而好文辭，又久無繼嗣，數為微行，多近幸小臣，趙、李從微賤專寵，皆皇太后與諸舅夙夜所常憂。

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng's nature was lenient and he loved literary phrasing; also long without heir, several times he made micro-journeys, drew near many petty favored ministers; Zhao and Li from mean base monopolized favor—all the Grand Empress Dowager and various maternal uncles night and day constantly worried.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng was mild and loved letters, long lacked an heir, often slipped out among petty favorites, while Zhao Feiyan and Li Ping from humble rank seized his love—the empress dowager and her brothers lost sleep over it.

sentences.zh: 至親難數言，故推永等使因天變而切諫，勸上納用之。

translations.literal: The nearest kin find it hard to speak often; therefore they pushed Yong and others to use Heaven's change for cutting remonstrance, urging the ruler to accept and use it.
translations.idiomatic: Kin could not speak plainly, so they pushed Gu Yong and others to use celestial portents as a lever for blunt advice the emperor might accept.

sentences.zh: 永自知有內應，展意無所依違，每言事輒見答禮。

translations.literal: Yong himself knew he had inner support; spreading intent he had nowhere to lean or shun—each time he spoke on affairs he always received courteous reply.
translations.idiomatic: Yong knew he had backers at court and never pulled his punches; each memorial won a polite hearing.

sentences.zh: 至上此對，上大怒。

translations.literal: When he reached this reply, the emperor was greatly angry.
translations.idiomatic: When this memorial arrived, the emperor flew into a rage.

sentences.zh: 衛將軍商密□永令發去。

translations.literal: General of the Guards Shang secretly □ Yong ordering him to set out and leave.
translations.idiomatic: General Wang Shang secretly told Yong to flee.

sentences.zh: 上使侍御史收永，敕過交道廄者勿追，御史不及永，還，上意亦解，自悔。

translations.literal: The emperor sent attendant censors to seize Yong, ordering those passing Jiaodao stable not to pursue; the censors did not catch up with Yong, returned; the emperor's intent also relaxed, himself repented.
translations.idiomatic: He sent censors after Yong but ordered the relay post not to give fresh mounts; they failed to overtake him, and the emperor's anger cooled into regret.

sentences.zh: 明年，征永為太中大夫，遷光祿大夫給事中。

translations.literal: The next year Yong was summoned as grandee of the palace, transferred to grand counselor of the palace with palace attendant status.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Yong was recalled as grandee of the palace, then promoted to grand counselor of the palace with concurrent palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 臣永幸得以愚朽之材為太中大夫，備拾遺之臣，從朝者之後，進不能盡思納忠輔宣聖德，退無被堅執銳討不義之功，猥蒙厚恩，仍遷至北地太過。

translations.literal: Your servant Yong is fortunate by mean rotten stuff to be grandee of the palace, filling the gleaning minister's post, following those who attend court—advancing unable fully to think and receive loyalty and aid in proclaiming sage virtue, retreating without wearing hard armor grasping sharp lance merit in punishing unrighteousness; basely receiving thick grace, still transferred to Beidi is too much.
translations.idiomatic: I am a worn-out man raised to grandee of the palace, a minor remonstrator who neither advances good counsel nor wins glory in arms; your kindness in moving me even to Beidi is more than I deserve.

sentences.zh: 絕命隕首，身膏野草，不足以報塞萬分。

translations.literal: Cutting off life and dashing out brains, body greasing the wild grass, is not enough to repay one ten-thousandth.
translations.idiomatic: Even to die in the grass would not repay one fraction of your grace.

sentences.zh: 陛下聖德寬仁，不遺易忘之臣，垂周文之聽，下及芻蕘之愚，有詔使衛尉受臣永所欲言。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's sage virtue is broad and kind, not casting away the easily forgotten minister, extending Zhou Wen's hearing down to fodder-cutting folly; there was an edict ordering the commandant of the guards to receive what your servant Yong wished to say.
translations.idiomatic: Your kindness does not forget the humble: like King Wen you listen even to woodcutters, and you ordered the commandant of the guards to hear me out.

sentences.zh: 臣聞事君之義，有言責者盡其忠，有官守者修其職。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard the duty of serving the ruler: those with remonstrance duty exhaust loyalty; those with office guard repair their posts.
translations.idiomatic: They say remonstrators owe full loyalty and office-holders owe diligent service.

sentences.zh: 臣永幸得免於言責之辜，有官守之任，當畢力遵職，養綏百姓而已，不宜復關得失之辭。

translations.literal: Your servant Yong is fortunate to escape the guilt of remonstrance duty, having office guard's charge—ought to exhaust strength following duty, nurturing and soothing the hundred surnames only, not fitting again to concern gain and loss words.
translations.idiomatic: I am no longer chief remonstrator but a line officer—I should devote myself to the people and not debate policy.

sentences.zh: 忠臣之於上，志在過厚，是故遠不違君，死不忘國。

translations.literal: The loyal minister toward the ruler: his intent aims at exceeding thickness; therefore far he does not disobey the ruler, in death he does not forget the state.
translations.idiomatic: A loyal minister never abandons his lord alive or dead, and never forgets the state.

sentences.zh: 昔史魚既沒，余忠未訖，委柩後寢，以屍達誠；

translations.literal: Anciently Shi Yu having died, surplus loyalty was not finished; he entrusted his bier in the rear chamber to convey sincerity with the corpse;
translations.idiomatic: Shi Yu, dying with counsel unheard, had his coffin wheeled into the inner room so his corpse could speak;

sentences.zh: 汲黯身外思內，發憤舒憂，遺言李息。

translations.literal: Ji An thought outside the body yet inside, venting anger and easing worry, leaving words to Li Xi.
translations.idiomatic: Ji An, though outwardly aloof, nursed grief for the state and left his last plea with Li Xi.

sentences.zh: 經曰：「雖爾身在外，乃心無不在王室。」

translations.literal: The classic says: "Though your body is outside, your heart is nowhere not in the royal house."
translations.idiomatic: The canon says, "Though your body be far off, your heart never leaves the royal house."

sentences.zh: 臣永幸得給事中出入三年，雖執干戈守邊垂，思慕之心常存於省闥，是以敢越郡吏之職，陳累年之憂。

translations.literal: Your servant Yong is fortunate to have been palace attendant in and out three years; though grasping halberds guarding the border fringe, longing thought always dwelt in the forbidden gates—therefore I dare overstep commandery clerk's duty, declaring piled years' worry.
translations.idiomatic: Though three years a palace attendant and now a frontier guard, my heart stayed at the palace gate—so I overstep my rank to voice years of worry.

sentences.zh: 臣聞天生蒸民，不能相治，為立王者以統理之，方制海內非為天子，列土封疆非為諸侯，皆以為民也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that Heaven bore the multitude, unable mutually to govern them, therefore set up a true king to unify and order them; squaring and ruling within the seas was not for the Son of Heaven's sake alone, apportioning land and sealing borders was not for feudal lords' sake—all was for the people.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven made the people and gave them kings to rule—not for the king's private glory but for the people's good.

sentences.zh: 垂三統，列三正，去無道，開有德，不私一姓，明天下乃天下之天下，非一人之天下也。

translations.literal: He hangs the Three Unities, arrays the Three Rectifiers, casts off the without-Way, opens the with-virtue, does not favor one surname, clarifying that the realm is the realm's realm, not one man's realm.
translations.idiomatic: He sets the three cosmic threads and three calendar beginnings, casts out the wicked and welcomes virtue, favors no single clan, and shows that All-under-Heaven belongs to All-under-Heaven, not to one man alone.

sentences.zh: 王者躬行道德，承順天地，博愛仁怒，恩及行葦，籍稅取民不過常法，宮室車服不逾制度，事節財足，黎庶和睦，則卦氣理效，五征時序，百姓壽考，庶草蕃滋，符瑞並降，以昭保右。

translations.literal: The true king personally practices virtue and the Way, receives and follows Heaven and Earth, broadly loves, humane and forgiving, grace reaching the walking reeds; land tax taken from the people does not pass constant law; palaces, chambers, chariots, robes do not exceed measure; affairs are ordered and wealth sufficient, black-haired multitude in harmony—then hexagram vapors align and respond, the five signs keep season, the hundred surnames reach old age, hundred plants flourish, tally and auspice descend together, thereby to display protection and aid.
translations.idiomatic: When the king walks in virtue, loves broadly, taxes lightly, builds within measure, and the people thrive, omens fall into harmony, the folk live long, and auspices gather to bless him.

sentences.zh: 失道妄行，逆天暴物，窮奢極欲，湛湎荒淫，婦言是從，誅逐仁賢，離逖骨肉，群小用事，峻刑重賦，百姓愁怨，則卦氣悖亂，咎徵著郵，上天震怒，災異屢降，日月薄食，五星失行，山崩川潰，水泉踴出，妖孽並見，□星耀光，饑饉荐臻，百姓短折，萬物夭傷。

translations.literal: Losing the Way and rashly acting, rebelling against Heaven and cruelly treating things, utmost extravagance and utmost desire, soaking and drowning in wasteness and excess, following women's words, executing and banishing benevolent worthies, estranging bone-and-flesh, petty men wielding affairs, harsh punishments and heavy levies, the hundred surnames sorrowful and resentful—then hexagram vapors are perverse and disordered, blame signs show in couriers, High Heaven blazes in anger, portents repeatedly descend, sun and moon press eclipse, five planets lose their courses, mountains collapse and rivers burst, springs gush upward, prodigies all appear, □ stars shine bright, famine comes again and again, the hundred surnames short-lived, myriad things wither young.
translations.idiomatic: When he defies Heaven, wastes the people, drowns in lust, obeys women, drives off worthies, trusts petty men, and crushes the folk with cruel law and tax, omens pile up—eclipses, wandering stars, flood, famine, early death, and every kind of baleful sign.

sentences.zh: 終不改寤，惡洽變備，不復譴告，更命有德。

translations.literal: To the end not reforming and waking, evil soaking and change complete, no longer sending reproving announcements, Heaven changes mandate to the virtuous.
translations.idiomatic: If he will not wake until evil is complete, Heaven ceases to warn and hands the Mandate to another.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：「乃眷四顧，此惟予宅。」

translations.literal: The "Odes" says: "Then he looked round the four quarters—this is my house."
translations.idiomatic: The "Odes" says the king looked about the four quarters and knew the realm was his house.

sentences.zh: 禍起細微，奸生所易。

translations.literal: Calamity rises from the fine; treachery grows from what is easy.
translations.idiomatic: Disaster begins in trifles; treason sprouts where guard is slack.

sentences.zh: 願陛下正君臣之義，無復與群小媟黷燕飲；

translations.literal: I wish Your Majesty to rectify lord and minister's righteousness, never again with crowds of petty men commit lewd defilement and banquet drinking;
translations.idiomatic: Set right the relation of lord and minister, and never again carouse in lewd company with petty men;

sentences.zh: 中黃門後庭素驕慢不謹嘗以醉酒失臣禮者，悉出勿留。

translations.literal: Among inner yellow gates and rear palace those by nature arrogant and slow, not careful, who once by drunk wine lost minister ritual—send them all out, do not keep them.
translations.idiomatic: Expel every arrogant palace attendant who has broken court etiquette in his cups.

sentences.zh: 勤三綱之嚴，修後宮之政，抑遠驕妒之憲，崇近婉順之行，加惠失志之人，懷柔怨恨之心。

translations.literal: Strengthen the three bonds' severity, repair inner palace government, check and distance arrogant jealousy's model, draw near yielding compliance's conduct, add grace to those who lost their will, embrace and soften resentful hearts.
translations.idiomatic: Strengthen the three bonds, order the harem, curb jealous favorites, cherish the gentle, comfort the slighted, and soothe bitter hearts.

sentences.zh: 保至尊之重，秉帝王之威，朝覲法出而後駕，陳兵清道而後行，無復輕身獨出，飲食臣妾之家。

translations.literal: Preserve the supreme weight, grasp emperor-king's awe; court audience and law issued then chariot moves, array troops and clear road then go; never again lightly body go alone, eating and drinking in ministers' and concubines' homes.
translations.idiomatic: Guard your majesty: issue your commands before the chariot rolls, clear the streets before you ride, and never again slip out alone to sup in servants' houses.

sentences.zh: 三者既除，內亂之路塞矣。

translations.literal: When these three are removed, the road of inner disorder is blocked.
translations.idiomatic: Remove these three evils and the inner court is sealed against chaos.

sentences.zh: 諸夏舉兵，萌在民饑饉而吏不恤，興於百姓困而賦斂重，發於下怨離而上不知。

translations.literal: When the various Xia raise arms, the sprout lies in the people's famine while clerks do not pity; it rises when the hundred surnames are trapped while levies are heavy; it breaks out when resentment below parts while above does not know.
translations.idiomatic: Rebellion in the heartland begins when famine goes unrelieved, taxes crush the poor, and the throne never hears their rage.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「屯其膏，小貞吉，大貞凶。」

translations.literal: The "Changes" says: "Hoard its fat—small divination auspicious, great divination inauspicious."
translations.idiomatic: The "Changes" warns: hoard the people's fat for small gain and you may survive a little probe, but the great cast is ill omened.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「饑而不損茲謂泰，厥災水，厥咎亡。」

translations.literal: Tradition says: "Hungry yet not reducing—this is called ease; its calamity water; its blame loss."
translations.idiomatic: Commentary says famine unmet with thrift is called false ease—it brings flood and ruin.

sentences.zh: 《訞辭》曰：「關動牡飛，辟為無道，臣為非，厥咎亂臣謀篡。」

translations.literal: The "Yaoci" says: "The pass stirs, the male bolt flies—the lord is without Way, ministers are not right; its blame disorderly ministers plotting usurpation."
translations.idiomatic: The "Yaoci" says when the pass bolts fly the ruler has lost the Way and ministers plot usurpation.

sentences.zh: 王者遭衰難之世，有饑饉之災，不損用而大自潤，故凶；

translations.literal: The true king who meets declining hard generations with famine calamity, not reducing use yet greatly enriching himself, therefore is inauspicious;
translations.idiomatic: In famine a king who enriches himself instead of cutting expenses courts ill fortune;

sentences.zh: 百姓困貧無以共求，愁悲怨恨，故水；

translations.literal: the hundred surnames trapped and poor have nothing to supply the demands, sorrowful and resentful—therefore water;
translations.idiomatic: when the people cannot meet your exactions, grief turns to flood;

sentences.zh: 城關守國之固，固將去焉，故牡飛。

translations.literal: city gates are the state's firmness; when firmness is about to depart, therefore the male bolt flies.
translations.idiomatic: the city gate is the state's lock—when bolts fly, the lock is going.

sentences.zh: 往年郡國二十一傷於水，災，禾黍不入。

translations.literal: In past years twenty-one commanderies were harmed by water; disaster, grain and millet did not enter.
translations.idiomatic: Last year twenty-one commanderies drowned; no grain was gathered.

sentences.zh: 今年蠶麥咸惡。

translations.literal: This year silkworm and wheat both are bad.
translations.idiomatic: This year silk and wheat alike failed.

sentences.zh: 百川沸騰，江河溢決，大水氾濫郡國五十有餘。

translations.literal: Hundred streams boil and surge, rivers overflow and burst; great water floods more than fifty commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Streams boil over, rivers burst their banks, and flood has swamped more than fifty commanderies.

sentences.zh: 比年喪稼，時過無宿麥。

translations.literal: Year after year crops are lost; the season passes with no winter wheat.
translations.idiomatic: Harvests fail year on year; the season passes with no winter wheat in the bin.

sentences.zh: 百姓失業流散，群輩守關。

translations.literal: The hundred surnames lose work and wander scattered; crowds guard the passes.
translations.idiomatic: The people are jobless and drift; mobs block the passes.

sentences.zh: 大異較炳如彼，水災浩浩，黎庶窮困如此，宜損常稅小自潤之時，而有司奏請加賦，甚繆經義，逆於民心，布怨趨禍之道也。

translations.literal: Great prodigies are so brilliantly manifest, water disaster so vast, black-haired so trapped and poor like this—it ought to be time to reduce regular tax and slightly enrich oneself, yet responsible offices memorialize requesting added levies—greatly wrong against classic meaning, contrary to the people's heart, the road of spreading resentment and rushing toward calamity.
translations.idiomatic: With omens so plain and the people so ruined, this is the moment to cut taxes—yet offices ask to raise them, a gross breach of the classics and an invitation to revolt.

sentences.zh: 牡飛之狀，殆為此發。

translations.literal: The condition of the male bolt flying is probably for this reason breaking out.
translations.idiomatic: The flying gate-bolts surely answer to this policy.

sentences.zh: 古者谷不登虧膳，災屢至損服，凶年不□塗，明王之制也《詩》云：「凡民有喪，扶服救之。」

translations.literal: Anciently when grain did not ripen they reduced meals; when disasters repeatedly came they cut garments; in bad years they did not □ the roads—this was the enlightened king's system. The "Odes" says: "Whenever the people have mourning, support and help them."
translations.idiomatic: The ancients cut court meals when crops failed and wore plain cloth when omens piled up; the "Odes" bids the ruler help the people in mourning.

sentences.zh: 《論語》曰：「百姓不足，君孰予足？」

translations.literal: The "Analects" says: "When the hundred surnames are insufficient, how does the ruler have enough?"
translations.idiomatic: The "Analects" asks: when the people are empty, what can fill the throne?

sentences.zh: 臣願陛下勿許加賦之奏，益減大官、導官、中御府、均官、掌畜、廩犧用度，止尚方、織室、京師郡國工服官發輸造作，以助大司農。

translations.literal: Your servant wishes Your Majesty not to approve added-levy memorials, rather increase reduction of Grand Provisioner, Conductor, Inner Treasury, Equalizer, Herd Keeper, Granary Sacrifice expenses, stop Upper Workshop, Weaving Office, capital and commandery work-clothes offices from dispatching and building, to aid the grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Reject higher taxes; slash spending on kitchens, workshops, the inner treasury, and imperial manufactories, and shift the savings to the minister of finance.

sentences.zh: 流恩廣施，振贍困乏，開關梁，內流民，恣所欲之，以救基急。

translations.literal: Spread grace broadly, relieve and aid the trapped and exhausted, open passes and bridges, admit wandering people, let them have what they wish—to rescue foundation's urgency.
translations.idiomatic: Open the roads, admit refugees, and spend freely to meet the crisis.

sentences.zh: 立春，遣使者循行風俗，宣佈聖德，存恤孤寡，問民所苦，勞二千石，敕勸耕桑，毋奪農時，以慰綏元元之心，防塞大奸之隙，諸夏之亂，庶幾可息。

translations.literal: At Beginning of Spring dispatch envoys to circuit customs and habits, proclaim sage virtue, preserve and pity widows and orphans, ask what the people suffer, labor the two-thousand-bushels, order and encourage farming and silkworms, do not seize farming seasons, thereby to soothe and settle the multitude's hearts, block and stop great wickedness's cracks—disorder in the various Xia perhaps can rest.
translations.idiomatic: At spring's start send inspectors to tour the realm, aid widows and orphans, urge the governors to farming, and never steal the plowing season—thus you calm the people and choke rebellion before it rises.

sentences.zh: 臣聞上主可與為善而不可與為惡，下主可與為惡而不可與為善。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard the highest ruler can be worked with for good but not for evil; the lowest ruler can be worked with for evil but not for good.
translations.idiomatic: The finest ruler can be led toward good, never toward evil; the basest the reverse.

sentences.zh: 陛下天然之性，疏通聰敏，上主之姿也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's inborn nature is open, penetrating, clever, and keen—the posture of the highest ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Your natural gifts are quick and clear—you have the makings of a supreme ruler.

sentences.zh: 少省愚臣之言，感寤三難，深畏大異，定心為善，捐忘邪志，毋二舊愆，厲精緻政，至誠應天，則積異塞於上，禍亂伏於下，何憂患之有？

translations.literal: If you slightly examine your foolish servant's words, are moved and wake to the three difficulties, deeply fear great prodigies, fix heart on good, cast away and forget evil intent, do not twice old faults, sharpen essence and order government, utmost sincerity answering Heaven—then piled prodigies will be blocked above, calamity and rebellion hide below—what worry or trouble would there be?
translations.idiomatic: Heed my three warnings, dread the omens, set your heart on reform, shed old vices, and govern with utmost sincerity toward Heaven—then portents will fade above and rebellion sink below.

sentences.zh: 竊恐陛下公志未專，私好頗存，尚愛群小，不肯為耳！

translations.literal: Your servant privately fears Your Majesty's public intent is not yet focused, private likes quite remain, still loving crowds of petty men, unwilling to do it only!
translations.idiomatic: I fear your public resolve still wavers, private tastes linger, and you still cling to petty men and will not act!

sentences.zh: 對奏，天子甚感其言。

translations.literal: When the reply was memorialized, the Son of Heaven was deeply moved by his words.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was deeply moved by the memorial.

sentences.zh: 永於經書，泛為疏達，與杜欽、杜鄴略等，不能洽浹如劉向父子及揚雄也。

translations.literal: Yong in the classics and documents was broadly rough and penetrating, about equal to Du Qin and Du Ye, not thorough and soaking like Liu Xiang father and son and Yang Xiong.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong's learning was broad but shallow compared with Du Qin, Du Ye, or the depth of Liu Xiang and his son and Yang Xiong.

sentences.zh: 其於天官、《京氏易》最密，故善言災異，前後所上四十餘事，略相反覆，專攻上身與後宮而已。

translations.literal: In celestial offices and "Jing's Changes" he was most dense; therefore he was good at speaking on portents; front and back he submitted more than forty matters, roughly repetitive, specializing in attacking the ruler's person and inner palace only.
translations.idiomatic: He mastered astronomy and Jing Fang's "Changes," so his forty-odd memorials on omens harped on the emperor and the harem.

sentences.zh: 黨於王氏，上亦知之，不甚親信也。

translations.literal: He factioned with the Wang clan; the emperor also knew it and did not greatly trust him.
translations.idiomatic: He leaned on the Wangs; the emperor knew it and kept him at arm's length.

sentences.zh: 杜鄴字子夏，本魏郡繁陽人也。

translations.literal: Du Ye, courtesy name Zixia, by origin was a man of Fanyang in Wei commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Du Ye, styled Zixia, was originally from Fanyang in Wei commandery.

sentences.zh: 祖父及父積功勞皆至郡守，武帝時徙茂陵。

translations.literal: Grandfather and father by accumulated merit both reached grand warden; in Emperor Wu's time they moved to Maoling.
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather and father rose on merit to grand warden and were moved to Maoling under Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 鄴少孤，其母張敞女。

translations.literal: Ye in youth was orphaned; his mother was Zhang Chang's daughter.
translations.idiomatic: Ye lost his father young; his mother was a daughter of Zhang Chang.

sentences.zh: 鄴。

translations.literal: Ye.
translations.idiomatic: His name was Ye, Du Ye.

sentences.zh: 鄴壯，從敞子吉學問，得其家書。

translations.literal: When Ye grew strong, he followed Chang's son Ji in study and inquiry, obtaining that family's books.
translations.idiomatic: As a grown man he studied under Zhang Ji and inherited the Zhang family library.

sentences.zh: 以孝廉以郎。

translations.literal: By filial and incorrupt he became gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Filial-incorrupt nomination won him a gentleman's post.

sentences.zh: 臣聞禽息憂國，碎首不恨；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard Qinxi worried for the state, dashed brains without regret;
translations.idiomatic: Qinxi broke his head for the state and felt no regret;

sentences.zh: 卞和獻寶，刖足願之。

translations.literal: Bian He presented the jewel, cut feet yet wished it.
translations.idiomatic: Bian He offered the jade though it cost him his feet;

sentences.zh: 臣幸得奉直言之詔，無二者之危，敢不極陳！

translations.literal: Your servant is fortunate to receive the edict for straight speech, without the two dangers—how dare I not exhaust statement!
translations.idiomatic: I am summoned to speak plainly without facing their fate—how can I hold back?

sentences.zh: 臣聞陽尊陰卑，卑者隨尊，尊者兼卑，天之道也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard yang is honored, yin is low; the low follows the honored, the honored embraces the low—it is Heaven's Way.
translations.idiomatic: Yang is high and yin low; the low follows the high—that is Heaven's Way.

sentences.zh: 是以男雖賤，各為其家陽；

translations.literal: Therefore though the male is low, each is yang for his household;
translations.idiomatic: Thus the man, though humble, is the yang of his house;

sentences.zh: 女雖貴，猶為其國陰。
