translations.literal: though the female is high, still she is yin for her state.
translations.idiomatic: the woman, though exalted, remains the yin of the state.

sentences.zh: 故禮明三從之義，雖有文母之德，必繫於子。

translations.literal: Therefore ritual clarifies the three followings' meaning; though she have Mother Wen's virtue, she must be tied to the son.
translations.idiomatic: Rite therefore binds even a woman of Tai Si's virtue to her son's line.

sentences.zh: 《春秋》不書紀侯之母，陰義殺也。

translations.literal: The "Spring and Autumn" does not record the mother of the Marquis of Ji—the yin meaning is repressed.
translations.idiomatic: The "Spring and Autumn" omits the mother of the marquis of Ji because a woman's power must not eclipse the yin role.

sentences.zh: 昔鄭伯隨姜氏之欲，終有叔段篡國之禍；

translations.literal: Anciently Duke Zhuang of Zheng followed Lady Jiang's desires, and in the end had the disaster of Shu Duan usurping the state;
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhuang of Zheng indulged his mother and reaped Shu Duan's revolt;

sentences.zh: 周襄王內迫惠後之難，而遭居鄭之危。

translations.literal: King Xiang of Zhou was inwardly pressed by Empress Hui's trouble, and met the peril of dwelling with Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: King Xiang of Zhou, squeezed by palace strife, fell into danger as a guest of Zheng.

sentences.zh: 漢興，呂太后權私親屬，又以外孫為孝惠後，是時繼嗣不明，凡事多暗，晝昏冬雷之變，不可勝載。

translations.literal: When Han rose, Empress Dowager Lu wielded power for private kin, and moreover made a maternal grandson Empress Xiaohui's empress—at that time the successor was unclear, all affairs mostly dark; day turned to dusk and winter thundered—changes beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: At Han's rise Empress Lü favored her clan and set her grandson on the heir's seat until succession was a muddle; omens darkened the court—dusk at noon, thunder in winter—too many to list.

sentences.zh: 竊見陛下行不偏之政，每事約儉，非禮不動，誠欲正身與天下更始也。

translations.literal: Your servant privately sees Your Majesty practicing impartial government, in each matter restrained and frugal, not moving without ritual—you truly wish to rectify the person and with All-under-Heaven make a fresh start.
translations.idiomatic: I see you govern even-handedly, live frugally, and never act without ritual—you mean to reform yourself and begin anew with the realm.

sentences.zh: 然嘉瑞未應，而日食、地震，民訛言行籌，傳相驚恐。

translations.literal: Yet fine auspice has not answered, while solar eclipse and earthquake come; the people spread rumors and counting-rods divination, passing alarm one to another.
translations.idiomatic: Yet no blessed omens answer; instead come eclipse and earthquake while the people whisper of divining rods and panic spreads.

sentences.zh: 案《春秋》災異，以指象為言語，故在於得一類而達之也。

translations.literal: Investigating the "Spring and Autumn" on disasters and prodigies, it takes pointing images as language; therefore it lies in obtaining one category and penetrating it.
translations.idiomatic: The "Spring and Autumn" reads each portent as a pointed image—one type unlocks the whole lesson.

sentences.zh: 日食，明陽為陰所臨，《坤卦》乘《離》，《明夷》之象也。

translations.literal: Solar eclipse means bright yang is overshadowed by yin—the hexagram Kun riding Li, the image of Mingyi.
translations.idiomatic: An eclipse means yin has veiled yang—Kun mounting Li, the "Brightness Wounded" sign.

sentences.zh: 《坤》以法地，為土為母，以安靜為德。

translations.literal: Kun takes law from earth, is earth and mother, takes quiet stillness as virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Kun stands for earth, the mother principle, whose virtue is stillness.

sentences.zh: 震，大陰之效也。

translations.literal: Quake is great yin's effect.
translations.idiomatic: The earthquake is the work of dominant yin.

sentences.zh: 占像甚明，臣敢不直言其事！

translations.literal: The divination image is very clear—your servant dares not fail to speak straight of the affair!
translations.idiomatic: The reading is plain—I must speak plainly!

sentences.zh: 昔曾子問從令之義，孔子曰：「是何言與！」

translations.literal: Anciently Zengzi asked the meaning of obeying orders; Confucius said: "What words are these!"
translations.idiomatic: When Zengzi asked about blind obedience, Confucius cried, "What talk is that!"

sentences.zh: 善閔子騫守禮不苟，從親所行，無非理者，故無可間也。

translations.literal: He praised Min Ziqian for guarding ritual without carelessness; what he followed from kin's conduct had nothing unreasonable—therefore there was nothing to drive between them.
translations.idiomatic: He praised Min Ziqian for keeping ritual: whatever his parents did stayed within reason, so no wedge could enter.

sentences.zh: 前大司馬新都侯莽退伏弟家，以詔策決，復遣就國。

translations.literal: Formerly Grand Marshal Marquis of Xindu Mang withdrew and lay low at his younger brother's house; by edict and tally the matter was decided, and he was again sent to his state.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang of Xindu once retired to his brother's house until an edict sent him back to his fief.

sentences.zh: 高昌侯宏去蕃自絕，猶受封土。

translations.literal: Marquis of Gaochang Hong left his fief and cut himself off, yet still received enfeoffed land.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Hong of Gaochang quit his fief yet kept his title.

sentences.zh: 制書侍中、駙馬都尉遷不忠巧佞，免歸故郡，間未旬月，則有詔還，大臣奏正其罰，卒不得遣，而反兼官奉使，顯寵過故。

translations.literal: Regulated edicts: attendant-in-chief and chief commandant of the consort cavalry Wang Qian was disloyal, crafty, and flattering—dismissed to his native commandery; within not yet ten months there was an edict recalling him; great ministers memorialized to correct his punishment, in the end could not get him dispatched, but on the contrary he held concurrent office and bore mission, displayed favor exceeding before.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Qian was cashiered for sycophancy, recalled within the month, never sent home despite ministers' protests, and ended with added titles and missions—more honored than before.

sentences.zh: 及陽信侯業，皆緣私君國，非功義所止。

translations.literal: As for Marquis of Yangxin Ye—all by private favor lorded over the state, not what merit and righteousness allow.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Ye of Yangxin likewise rode private favor to power beyond what merit allows.

sentences.zh: 諸外家昆弟無賢不肖，並侍帷幄，布在列位，或典兵衛，或將軍屯，寵意並於一家，積貴之勢，世所稀見所稀聞也。

translations.literal: The various outer kin, brothers, without regard to worthy or unworthy, together attend the curtain, spread through the ranks, some commanding guards, some leading encampments; favoring intent joins on one house—piled eminence's force is what the age rarely sees and rarely hears.
translations.idiomatic: Every in-law brother, worthy or not, sits by the throne, fills every post, commands troops or camps—favor heaps on one clan as seldom seen in history.

sentences.zh: 至乃並置大司馬、將軍之官。

translations.literal: It reached even to jointly establishing Grand Marshal and general offices.
translations.idiomatic: It went so far as twin offices of Grand Marshal and general.

sentences.zh: 皇甫雖盛，三桓雖隆，魯為作三軍，無以甚此。

translations.literal: Though the Huang clans were flourishing, though the Three Huan were lofty, Lu for them raised three armies—nothing surpasses this.
translations.idiomatic: The Huang and Three Huan never matched this: even Lu's triple host was a lesser climb.

sentences.zh: 當拜之日，暗然日食。

translations.literal: On the day of the investiture, dimly a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The day of the appointments the sun went dark.

sentences.zh: 不在前後，臨事而發者，明陛下謙遜無專，承指非一，所言輒聽，所欲輒隨，有罪惡者不坐辜罰，無功能者畢受官爵，流漸積猥，正尤在是，欲令昭昭以覺聖朝。

translations.literal: Not before or after, breaking out at the affair—it shows Your Majesty's humility and lack of monopoly, receiving pointers not from one alone: what is spoken is always heard, what is desired is always followed; those with guilt and wickedness do not sit for crime and punishment, those without merit and ability all receive office and nobility—flow gradually accumulates filth, the blame lies especially here, wishing to make it brilliantly clear to wake the sagely court.
translations.idiomatic: The eclipse fell on the very day of the promotions—Heaven shows you take counsel from every voice, grant every wish, spare the guilty, and ennoble the useless until corruption pools; the omen points straight at this, begging the court to wake.

sentences.zh: 昔詩人所刺，《春秋》所譏，指象如此，殆不在它。

translations.literal: What the poets satirized, what the "Spring and Autumn" ridiculed—pointing the image thus, probably is not elsewhere.
translations.idiomatic: The poets mocked it and the "Spring and Autumn" condemned the like—Heaven points here, nowhere else.

sentences.zh: 由後視前，忿邑非之，逮身所行，不自鏡見，則以為可，計之過者。

translations.literal: Viewing the later and despising the former, resentful and cramped—coming to what oneself practices, not seeing oneself in the mirror, then thinking it acceptable is a fault of reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: Men hate past favorites yet never hold a mirror to their own acts—thinking themselves blameless is the true error.

sentences.zh: 疏賤獨偏見，疑內亦有此類。

translations.literal: A mean and low man alone sees biasedly; he suspects within there is also this kind.
translations.idiomatic: A humble outsider may see one-sidedly, yet I suspect the inner court harbors the same disease.

sentences.zh: 天變不空，保右世主如此之至，奈何不應！

translations.literal: Heaven's change is not empty; it protects and aids the age's ruler to this utmost—how can you not respond!
translations.idiomatic: Heaven does not warn in vain; it guards the sovereign so faithfully—how can you not answer!

sentences.zh: 臣聞野雞著怪，高宗深動；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that when wild pheasants showed prodigy, Gaozong was deeply moved;
translations.idiomatic: When wild pheasants foretold the Shang restoration, Gaozong took fright;

sentences.zh: 大風暴過，成王怛然。

translations.literal: when a great wind violently passed, King Cheng was alarmed.
translations.idiomatic: when the gale tore the mulberry grove, King Cheng trembled.

sentences.zh: 願陛下加致精誠，思承始初，事稽諸古，以厭下心，則黎庶群生無不說喜，上帝百神收還威怒，禎祥福祿何嫌不報！

translations.literal: I wish Your Majesty would add utmost sincerity, think to inherit the beginning, examine affairs against antiquity, thereby to satisfy those below—then the black-haired multitude will none fail to rejoice; High God and the hundred spirits will gather back awesome anger—how would auspicious blessing grudge not to answer!
translations.idiomatic: Add utmost sincerity, think back to your first intent, test policy against the classics, and win the people's hearts—then the spirits will lay aside wrath and blessings will return without stint.

sentences.zh: 鄴未拜，病卒。

translations.literal: Before Ye was invested, he died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Du Ye died of illness before he could take office.

sentences.zh: 鄴言民訛言行籌，及谷永言王者買私田，彗星隕石牡飛之占，語在《五行志》。

translations.literal: Ye's words on the people's rumor and counting rods, and Gu Yong's words on the king buying private fields, comet, meteor, and male-bolt flight divinations—the account is in the "Treatise on the Five Phases."
translations.idiomatic: Du Ye on the rod divinations and Gu Yong on the emperor's private land, comets, meteors, and flying gate-bolts are recorded in the Treatise on the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 初，鄴從張吉學，吉子竦又幼孤，從鄴學問，亦著於世，尤長小學。

translations.literal: At first Ye studied with Zhang Ji; Ji's son Song was also young orphan, followed Ye in study and inquiry, and was also illustrious in the age, especially long in "minor learning."
translations.idiomatic: Ye had studied under Zhang Ji; Ji's orphaned son Song studied under Ye in turn and won fame, especially in philology.

sentences.zh: 鄴於林，清靜好古，亦有雅材，建武中歷位列卿，至大司空。

translations.literal: Ye's son Lin was quiet, fond of antiquity, and also had refined talent; during Jianwu he passed through ranked ministers to Grand Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: His son Du Lin loved antiquity and had talent; under Guangwu he rose to Grand Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 其正文字過於鄴、竦，故世言小學者由杜公。

translations.literal: He corrected graphs and characters surpassing Ye and Song; therefore the age says "minor learning" derives from Lord Du.
translations.idiomatic: He surpassed father and master in textual criticism, so later scholars trace philology to the Du house.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：孝成之世，委政外家，諸舅持權，重於丁、傅在孝哀時。

translations.literal: The praise says: In Emperor Cheng's era he entrusted government to outer kin; the various maternal uncles held power, heavier than Ding and Fu at Emperor Ai's time.
translations.idiomatic: The summation: Chengdi handed the realm to in-laws whose grip exceeded the Ding and Fu clans under Aidi.

sentences.zh: 故杜鄴敢譏丁、傅，而欽、永不敢言王氏，其勢然也。

translations.literal: Therefore Du Ye dared ridicule Ding and Fu, while Qin and Yong did not dare speak of the Wang clan—the situation was thus.
translations.idiomatic: So Du Ye could mock the Ding and Fu, while Du Qin and Gu Yong kept silent on the Wangs—such was the force of circumstance.

sentences.zh: 及欽欲挹損鳳權，而鄴附會音、商。

translations.literal: When Qin wished to check and reduce Feng's power, Ye attached and aligned with Yin and Shang.
translations.idiomatic: When Du Qin tried to check Wang Feng, Du Ye curried Wang Yin and Wang Shang instead.

sentences.zh: 永陳三七之戒，斯為忠焉，至其引申伯以阿鳳，隙平阿於車騎，指金、火以求合，可謂諒不足而談有餘者。

translations.literal: Yong stated the three-seven warning—this was loyal; as to extending Shen Bo to flatter Feng, alienating Ping'e against the chariot-and-cavalry commander, pointing to metal and fire to seek accord—may be called sincerity insufficient while talk excessive.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong's "three-seven" warning was true loyalty; yet he cited Shen Bo to flatter Wang Feng, sowed strife with Ping'e, and read Venus and Mars to suit his patrons—candor thin, words many.

sentences.zh: 孔子稱「友多聞」，三人近之矣。

translations.literal: Confucius called "friends much hearing"—these three men neared it.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius praised the friend rich in counsel—these three came close to that mark.
